reasoning: The primary swimming season for long course (50m) concludes in August (with the 2025 World Championships having taken place in Singapore in August). The period from late October through January is predominantly focused on short course (25m) competitions, including the World Cup series and the Short Course World Championships typically held in December. Therefore, there are very few elite-level long course competitions during these months where a competitive taper would occur. Lilly King's 100m breaststroke world record of 1:04.13 has stood since July 2017. An 8-year-old world record has a high base rate of survival over any given 3-month stretch, and survival is even more probable during the off-season for long course. While competitors like Tang Qianting have posted times relatively close to the record in recent years, the lack of major long course events makes it highly unlikely the record will be broken before January 24, 2026. Uncertainty increases slightly as the time horizon extends to late January due to the possibility of unexpected regional long-course meets or early-season tapers.
